channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Sam Altman] LIVE: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Speaks at BlackRock Infrastructure Summit | DRM News | AI1F
item.author: DRM News
item.description:
DRM News · 39 min
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman speaks live at the BlackRock U.S. Infrastructure Summit in Washington, D.C. Leaders from government, technology, and finance gathered to discuss investment, infrastructure development, and the role of emerging technologies in economic growth. The recording captures Altman's live remarks on rapid AI adoption in business and society, the scaling of OpenAI's infrastructure, and his vision of intelligence as a widely accessible utility.

item.title: [Yann LeCun] "LLMs Are A Dead End": An Exclusive Interview With The Genius Father of AI | Yann LeCun
item.author: This Is The World
item.description:
This Is The World · 51 min
An exclusive interview with Yann LeCun, chief AI scientist at Meta and Turing Award laureate. Recorded in the wake of his announcement of AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs), a new $1 billion venture focused on world models, LeCun discusses why he believes large language models are a dead end for achieving true intelligence, and what he sees as the more promising path forward in AI research.

item.title: [Geoffrey Hinton] The Man Who Built AI Says We Should Be Worried | Geoffrey Hinton (The Diary Of A CEO)
item.author: The Lumen Project
item.description:
The Lumen Project · 27 min
Geoffrey Hinton, widely known as the「Godfather of AI,」speaks about the risks of the artificial intelligence technology he spent over 50 years helping to develop. Originally from The Diary of a CEO, Hinton discusses autonomous weapons, AI-driven cyber attacks, and why he believes humanity should take the existential risks of AI seriously — explaining how the technology that transformed the world could also become one of its greatest threats.

item.title: [Andrew Ng] EducAIted Voices: Dr. Andrew Ng, Exploring Computer Science & K12 Education
item.author: EducAIted
item.description:
EducAIted · 28 min
Dr. Andrew Ng, founder of DeepLearning.AI and a leading AI educator, joins the premiere episode of EducAIted Voices — an interview series for K-12 educators navigating the AI era. Hosted by Emma Ludwick and Dr. Bailey Ubellacker, the conversation covers computer science education, building AI literacy among students, and practical guidance for educators adapting to rapidly shifting AI tools in and out of the classroom.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] On the Road With DRIVE AV
item.author: NVIDIA
item.description:
NVIDIA · 23 min
NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang takes a drive through San Francisco with NVIDIA VP of Automotive Xinzhou Wu, discussing what it takes to deliver autonomous driving that feels comfortable, confident, and safe. The conversation covers NVIDIA's DRIVE AV platform, the technical requirements for scalable Level 4 autonomy, and the road to viable robotaxis.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang and the Reinvention of Computing
item.author: APPLIED ENGLISH LABS
item.description:
APPLIED ENGLISH LABS · 51 min
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang discusses the evolution of accelerated computing and the strategic decisions that positioned NVIDIA at the center of the AI revolution. He traces how early GPU innovations for video games provided the parallel processing foundation for modern deep learning breakthroughs, and looks ahead to physical AI — digital twins, world models, humanoid robotics, and factory automation — as the next wave of computing reinvention.

item.title: [Topic: Google DeepMind Talks] 10 years of AlphaGo: The turning point for AI | Thore Graepel & Pushmeet Kohli
item.author: Google DeepMind
item.description:
Google DeepMind · 54 min
Ten years after AlphaGo's landmark victory over Go world champion Lee Sedol in Seoul (March 2016), DeepMind researchers Thore Graepel and Pushmeet Kohli reflect on how that moment changed AI history. The conversation traces AlphaGo's development using reinforcement learning, the move 37 that surprised the world, its broader impact on the field, and what the decade since has meant for AI research and society.

item.title: [Channel: Dwarkesh Patel] Dylan Patel — The single biggest bottleneck to scaling AI compute
item.description:
None · 151 min
Dylan Patel, founder of SemiAnalysis, joins Dwarkesh Patel for a deep dive into the three major bottlenecks constraining AI compute scaling: logic chips, memory, and power. The conversation examines the economics of AI labs, hyperscalers, semiconductor foundries, and fab equipment manufacturers, walking through every level of the AI compute stack to understand where the constraints lie and what it will take to overcome them.

item.title: [Channel: Machine Learning Street Talk] When AI Discovers the Next Transformer — Robert Lange
item.description:
None · 78 min
Robert Lange, founding researcher at Sakana AI, joins Tim Scarfe on Machine Learning Street Talk to discuss Shinka Evolve — a framework combining large language models with evolutionary algorithms for open-ended program search. The core argument: systems like AlphaEvolve can optimize solutions to fixed problems, but real scientific progress requires co-evolving the problems themselves. The conversation explores what it would take for AI to autonomously discover the next foundational architecture like the Transformer.
